channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Estonia’s defense chief: Send Ukraine money, not foreign troops
item.description: HALIFAX, Nova Scotia — Ukraine needs money invested into its own military production industry, not foreign troops to fight its war against Russia, Estonia’s Minister of Defense Hanno Pevkur told The Hill.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Bah, humbug! Vandal smashes Ebenezer Scrooge's tombstone used in 'A Christmas Carol' movie
item.description: If life imitates art, a vandal in the English countryside may be haunted by The Ghost of Christmas Yet to Come.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: French mass rape trial prosecutors demand maximum sentence for Gisèle Pelicot's ex-husband
item.description: AVIGNON, France (AP) — A mammoth rape trial in France moved into a new phase Monday as prosecutors began to lay out the verdicts and punishments they want for dozens of men accused of raping Gisèle Pelicot while she was drugged and rendered unconscious by her husband.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER and MARINE LESPRIT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DHL cargo plane crashes and skids into a house in Lithuania, killing a Spanish crew member
item.description: VILNIUS, Lithuania (AP) — A DHL cargo plane crashed on approach to an airport in Lithuania's capital and skidded into a house Monday morning, killing a Spanish crew member but not harming anyone on the ground. The cause is under investigation.
item.dc:creator: SIARHEI SATSIUK, VANESSA GERA and JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel 'moving forward' on possible Hezbollah cease-fire, official says
item.description: The Israeli Security Cabinet will convene Tuesday to discuss a possible cease-fire agreement with Hezbollah terrorists in Lebanon, Ambassador Danny Danon said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Scuffles in Serbian parliament as deadly station collapse sparks anger at the government
item.description: Scuffles and fistfights broke out between ruling party and opposition lawmakers in Serbia’s parliament on Monday over a deadly rail station roof collapse that has ignited tensions in the Balkan state.
item.dc:creator: Dusan Stojanovic, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 1 dead after DHL cargo plane crashes in Lithuania; officials 'cannot rule out' terrorism
item.description: Lithuanian officials said they were investigating the cause of the crash, and "cannot rule out" terrorism.
item.dc:creator: Siarhei Satsiuk, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Hundreds of homeowners in England and Wales battle floodwaters after weekend storm
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Hundreds of homeowners in England and Wales were battling floodwaters Monday morning after the second major storm of the winter brought widespread disruption to the U.K.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Egyptian officials say 16 people are missing after a tourist yacht sank in high waves on the Red Sea
item.description: Egyptian officials say a tourist yacht has sunk in the Red Sea after warnings of rough waters and 16 people are missing.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A far-right populist shocks Romania by heading into the presidential runoff
item.description: Romania is reeling after a little-known, far-right populist secured the first round in the presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Mcgrath, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Uruguay’s leftist opposition candidate Yamandú Orsi becomes country's new president
item.description: MONTEVIDEO, Uruguay (AP) — Uruguay's leftist opposition candidate, Yamandú Orsi, became the country's new president in a tight runoff Sunday, ousting the conservative governing coalition and making the South American nation the latest to rebuke the incumbent party in a year of landmark elections wor
item.dc:creator: NAYARA BATSCHKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korea holds memorial for forced laborers at Sado mines, a day after boycotting Japanese event
item.description: SADO, Japan (AP) — South Korea paid tribute to wartime Korean forced laborers at Japan’s Sado Island Gold Mines in a memorial ceremony on Monday, a day after boycotting a similar event organized by Japan, as tensions over historical atrocities continue to strain relations between the two sides.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lebanon's Shiite Muslims pay high price in war between Israel and Hezbollah
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — The Lebanese civilians most devastated by the Israel- Hezbollah war are Shiite Muslims, and many of them believe they are being unfairly punished because they share a religious identity with Hezbollah militants and often live in the same areas.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE and KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: G7 foreign ministers push for Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire in final G7 of Biden administration
item.description: Foreign ministers from the world’s leading industrialized nations are expressing cautious optimism about possible progress on a ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Winfield, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Moscow offers debt forgiveness to new recruits and AP sees wreckage of a new Russian missile
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russian President Vladimir Putin has signed a law granting debt forgiveness to new army recruits who enlist to fight in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB and ELISE MORTON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: U.N. reaches agreement on $300 billion climate deal for developing countries
item.description: The United Nations reached a $300 billion climate agreement for developing countries as they grapple with the significant effects of climate change. Delegates agreed Saturday to the billion-dollar pledge after “weeks of intense negotiations,” the U.N. said.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Scientists study ‘very rare’ frozen remains of 35,000-year-old saber-toothed cub
item.description: Scientists studied a three-week-old saber-toothed cub dating back 35,000 that had been left almost perfectly intact, thanks to its frozen condition.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A look inside the most expensive house in the world — the home of the UK’s monarch
item.description: Buckingham Palace in London is worth about $4.9 billion and has about 775 rooms, including royal and guest rooms, offices and bathrooms.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Catherine Herridge wants Trump 'sit-down' on bipartisan press bill
item.description: “This case involves the First Amendment and the right of reporters in the future,” Catherine Herridge said.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Brazil’s former President Bolsonaro and aides indicted for alleged 2022 coup attempt
item.description: Former Brazilian President Jair Bolsonaro and 36 others, including aides, were indicted by federal police on Thursday for allegedly attempting a coup to keep him in office in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US briefed Ukraine ahead of Putin's 'experimental Intermediate-range ballistic' attack
item.description: The U.S. alerted Ukraine and other allies ahead of Russia's attack that Moscow might use this "experimental" ballistic weapon to help Kyiv prepare ahead of the attack on Dnipro, a U.S. official confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Admiral: Middle East, Ukraine conflicts strain US air defense stocks
item.description: Conflicts in the Middle East and Ukraine are adding a strain to the U.S. military’s ability to respond to China, a top U.S. admiral warns.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: US vetoes UN resolution demanding a cease-fire in Gaza because there's no link to a hostage release
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The United States on Wednesday vetoed a U.N. Security Council resolution demanding an immediate cease-fire in the war in Gaza because it was not linked to an immediate release of hostages taken captive by Hamas militants in Israel in October 2023.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, WGN 9
